title: cereus jamacaru subsp. goiasensis
intro: Cereus jamacaru subsp. goiasensis is a cactus species native to Brazil, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including wound healing, antiviral and antitumor properties, as well as potential treatments for diabetes and hypertension. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
